ground_truth.title: 7 Plumbing Industry Trends You Need To Know in 2025
ground_truth.author: Podium Staff
ground_truth.main_content: We'll always need plumbers to maintain water systems as vital infrastructure, but the volume and type of plumbers' work is impacted by trends in the construction industry and consumer demand. The foundation of a successful plumbing business is knowing how to help customers choose you proactively, staying ahead of competitors by understanding factors influencing the construction industry, plumbing companies, and customer preferences. Homeowners are most likely to need plumbing services to fix current systems, upgrade old systems, or add new systems as part of home improvement projects, while commercial entities provide work through new construction and maintenance of existing buildings. The economic situation has slowed new construction, but homeowners are renovating to better meet their needs without moving, and customers are willing to invest in plumbing fixtures that align with smart technology and sustainability trends. Smart technology allows homeowners to manage their homes from phones through interconnected networks of sensors, IoT devices, and machine learning algorithms for wireless control, automated routines, and advanced monitoring to track water usage and flag potential issues. Green plumbing fulfills the high-priority customer desire for sustainability through low-flow toilets, water-saving faucets, connections to sustainable energy sources, and greywater systems that collect and reuse clean water. The plumbing industry faces labor shortages with an estimated 5% job growth over the next 10 years, and there is specific need for workers skilled in smart technology and eco-friendly plumbing as major drivers of growth. New construction will pick up, raw material costs will ease, and new technology will make plumbing systems and job execution more efficient, with the global plumbing market enjoying a CAGR of over 5.4%.
with: We'll always need plumbers, right? Well yes, we need professional plumbers to maintain water systems. It's vital infrastructure.
The foundation of a successful business is knowing how to help your customers choose you.
Smart technology allows for wireless control to turn appliances on and off, automate routines, and access advanced monitoring to track water usage and flag potential issues.
Consumers are very aware of their impact on the planet and want plumbing solutions to reduce damage and support sustainability.
The plumber job outlook is estimated to increase by 5% over the next 10 years.
